text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is reliable and easy to use. First, keep endpoints clear and consistent. For example, use nouns like `/users` or `/orders` instead of actions. Proper status codes are also essential: return `200` for success, `201` for created resources, and `404` for not found. Always include versioning in your API. You can do this by adding a version number to the URL, such as `/v1/users`. This way, updates won’t break existing clients. Finally, provide thorough documentation. Explain each endpoint, its parameters, and expected responses. Good documentation helps developers integrate quickly and avoid errors.
